contrastLeft.definition: Contrast of left-side stimulus in [0, 1]; NaN if the stimulus is on the other side.

contrastRight.definition: Contrast of right-side stimulus in [0, 1]; NaN if the stimulus is on the other side.

choice.definition: Response ID: -1 = turn CCW / left, +1 = turn CW / right, 0 = no-go.

feedbackType.definition: Feedback sign: +1 = positive / reward (correct), -1 = negative (error or no-go), 0 = no feedback.

probabilityLeft.definition: Hidden block prior: probability that the stimulus appears on the left (from stim_probability_left). Biased-choice sessions typically use {0.2, 0.5, 0.8}.

stimOn_times.definition: Stimulus onset time in seconds relative to session start.

goCue_times.definition: Go-cue tone time in seconds relative to session start.

response_times.definition: Time when a response was recorded (end of closed-loop state), in seconds relative to session start.

feedback_times.definition: Time of feedback delivery (reward or error noise), in seconds relative to session start.

rewardVolume.definition: Volume of reward delivered on each trial (µl); 0 if incorrect.

firstMovement_times.definition: Time of first movement in seconds relative to session start.

intervals_0.definition: Trial start time (first column of trials.intervals).

intervals_1.definition: Trial end time (second column of trials.intervals).

goCueTrigger_times.definition: Time the go-cue trigger command was sent (may precede measured goCue_times).

stimOnTrigger_times.definition: Time the stimulus-onset trigger command was sent.

stimOff_times.definition: Stimulus offset time in seconds relative to session start.

quiescencePeriod.definition: Required quiescence duration before the trial proceeds.

project_derived_encodings.stimulus_right: Binary channel: 1 if stim on right (contrastRight finite), else 0. Not an IBL column.
project_derived_encodings.contrast_high: Binary high/low contrast split of absolute contrast. Cutoff is a project decision; IBL only stores continuous contrastLeft/Right.
project_derived_encodings.delay_phase: Within-trial binary phase channel for RNN event bins. Project-defined.
project_derived_encodings.response_made: Within-trial binary event at response. Project-defined.
project_derived_encodings.reward: Binary reward-present channel. May be derived from feedbackType==1 or rewardVolume>0; incorrect-as-separate-channel is still open.
project_derived_encodings.prev_fast_rt: Binary previous-RT summary (below session median). Project-defined; uses derived RT, not an IBL column.
project_derived_encodings.oracle_prior_right: Full-information oracle prior channel from probabilityLeft. Project-defined binarization of an IBL field.
project_derived_encodings.rt_target: Training target: response_times - goCue_times (fallback stimOn). Project-defined from IBL times.

stimulus_side.ibl_rule: Stim on left iff contrastLeft is finite and contrastRight is NaN; stim on right iff contrastRight is finite and contrastLeft is NaN.

stimulus_contrast.ibl_rule: Absolute contrast = non-NaN side value in [0, 1]. IBL also uses signed contrast = contrastRight - contrastLeft (left negative) via brainbox.behavior.training.get_signed_contrast.

correctness_or_reward.ibl_rule: feedbackType +1 correct/reward, -1 error/no-go, 0 no feedback; rewardVolume > 0 only on rewarded trials.

block_prior.ibl_rule: probabilityLeft is the true hidden prior P(stim left). Biased blocks typically in {0.2, 0.5, 0.8}.

response_time.ibl_rule: Absolute session time when response was recorded.

feedback_time.ibl_rule: Absolute session time of reward or error feedback.

reaction_time_ingredients.ibl_rule: IBL provides the ingredient times only. Project RT target is derived: response_times - goCue_times, fallback response_times - stimOn_times.

contrastLeft.ibl_definition: Contrast of left-side stimulus in [0, 1]; NaN if the stimulus is on the other side.

contrastRight.ibl_definition: Contrast of right-side stimulus in [0, 1]; NaN if the stimulus is on the other side.

notes: Discovery only; does not freeze the preprocessing contract.
Raw IBL field definitions come from IBL docs/extractors, not the /data portal page.
RT preview is project-derived: goCue→response with stimOn→response fallback.
Contrast preview uses abs contrast on the non-NaN stimulated side.
Project-derived encodings (contrast_high, prev_fast_rt, etc.) are listed separately.
